v1.post_edit: sign-off restored to 'AAAI Team' (reduce over-generalized to 'Conference Team')

v2.method: manual curation of v1 per review (see Claude.md Phase 7C/7D)
changes: INVERTED v1 rules 13-14: never claim an action was taken (drafter cannot act); phrase operational steps as pending
refusal rules made conditional on retrieved context (outcome from retrieval, phrasing from guide)
dropped distillation-artifact constraints (helpdesk/merge/typo rules the drafter never encounters)
dropped edge-case voice rules (informal greetings, internal confirmations)
single unified grounding constraint instead of overlapping restatements
no hard word cap (was 'usually under 150 words') — length is the model's call, but concise/to-the-point/no-filler kept (user direction 2026-07-17)
v2.eval_plan: A/B v1 vs v2 (vs no guide) in the held-out eval against real chair replies once retrieval bench + drafter auth fix land
revision_2026_07_17_placeholder_contract: rule 2.1 rewritten: never PROMISE an action either ('we will look into...'); operational steps become [CHAIR: ...] placeholders
hard constraint 1 rewritten: unanswerable parts get an inline [CHAIR: ...] placeholder + a chair note, never 'the policy does not specify' in the reply

motivation: 50/74 eval drafts leaked chair-facing meta language into the reply body; the drafter system prompt now carries the same placeholder contract (primary enforcement) and this guide must not contradict it
revision_2026_07_17_contract_style_split: principle: system prompt = contract (holds with any/no guide, code-enforced); guide = voice (Marc-corpus conventions, swappable/A-B-able)

removed as duplicated-or-contract: 2.1 (action claims/promises -> system prompt), all of section 3 (grounding + placeholder mechanics were duplicates; no-real-names and no-past-cycles moved to system prompt)
added scope + subordination header (restores the clause _system_prompt()'s docstring relies on, lost in the v1->v2 curation); '[Sender name] exactly as written' kept in section 1
behavioral rules renumbered 2-8 -> 1-7; guide ~40% shorter; guide-vs-none ablation is now voice-only
revision_2026_07_17_greeting_population: greeting: address the requester by the name their email gives (sender line or signature); role-based greeting when unknown; never a placeholder/<name> token (user decision)
[Sender name] sign-off stays a literal placeholder for now; to be auto-populated from the authenticated chair account once the account system lands
companion change outside the guide: eval/test scripts stopped scrubbing ticket text — the system must be tested on exact emails

note: Guides are style/behavior only; policy content comes from retrieval. Active version to be selected by the future STYLE_GUIDE_PATH setting.
